channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Gonzaga beats No. 19 Saint Mary's 58-51 to capture West Coast Conference Tournament title
item.description: Braden Huff scored 18 points and Gonzaga avenged two regular-season losses to Saint Mary’s to beat the 19th-ranked Gaels 58-51 in the West Coast Conference Tournament championship.
item.dc:creator: Mark Anderson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bills agree to sign edge rusher Joey Bosa to 1-year, $12.6 million contract, AP source says
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — The Buffalo Bills turned to Joey Bosa to fill their pass-rush needs, agreeing with him on a one-year, $12.6 million contract, a person familiar with the deal told The Associated Press on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tyrese Haliburton's stunning 4-point play gives the Pacers a 115-114 win over the Bucks
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Tyrese Haliburton made a fadeaway 3-pointer while being fouled and completed the four-point play with 3.4 seconds left, and the Indiana Pacers stunned Giannis Antetokounmpo and the Milwaukee Bucks 115-114 on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: MARK AMBROGI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: NBA-leading Cavaliers beat Nets 109-104 for their second 15-game win streak of season
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — All-Star Darius Garland scored 18 of his 30 points in the fourth quarter and the NBA-leading Cleveland Cavaliers rallied for a 109-104 victory over the Brooklyn Nets on Tuesday night, matching the longest winning streak in franchise history at 15 games.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN DULIK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump admin cuts funding to Maine universities as state defies order to ban trans athletes from women's sports
item.description: The USDA announced Tuesday it is halting federal funding to the University of Maine System due to the state's ongoing battle for transgender athlete inclusion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Junior Bridgeman, businessman and basketball standout for Louisville and Milwaukee Bucks, dies at 71
item.description: Junior Bridgeman, a basketball standout who went on to an even more successful career in business, has died at the age of 71.
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schreiner, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Big 12 commissioner believes NCAA Tournament expansion is due. He says 76 teams is the right number
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Big 12 commissioner Brett Yormark is in favor of expanding the men's NCAA Tournament to 76 teams, and he believes a decision could come in the next few months, opening the possibility of proposed changes being implemented as soon as next year.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Warriors' Steph Curry becomes investor in Unrivaled Basketball
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- Stephen Curry has joined Unrivaled basketball as an investor. The Golden State Warriors guard is the latest sports star to join the 3v3 basketball league as an investor, joining the likes of the NBA's Giannis Antetokounmpo and Carmelo Anthony, and tennis star Coco Gauff.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Ex-Giants quarterback Daniel Jones to sign with Colts on 1-year deal: reports
item.description: Former New York Giants quarterback Daniel Jones and the Indianapolis Colts agreed to terms on a one-year deal, according to reports on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Najee Harris agrees to 1-year deal with Chargers: reports
item.description: Najee Harris and the Los Angeles Chargers have reportedly agreed to a one-year deal worth up to $9.5 million, as the ex-Pittsburgh Steeler finds a new home out west.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Several once-highly ranked teams enter conference tournament week in need of wins to make NCAA field
item.description: LAWRENCE, Kan. (AP) — This was a time early in the season when Kansas seemed to be an insurmountable juggernaut, full of high-profile transfers such as Zeke Mayo and AJ Storr, All-American candidates such as Hunter Dickinson and veterans accustomed to winning. They were the preseason No.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More than basketball: UCSD's Division I high tide lifts all athletic boats
item.description: While the great work of the 28-4 UCSD basketball team is the headline, the decade-long journey of the Tritons athletic program to D1 status is the work that athletic director Earl Edwards prides himself on.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Stone, Fox 5

item.title: Gilgeous-Alexander, Jokic play to a draw in back-to-back games between top MVP candidates
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and Nikola Jokic, two of the favorites for the NBA's MVP award, showed why in a pair of exciting games on back-to-back days in Oklahoma City. Neither emerged as a clear favorite to claim the hardware.
item.dc:creator: CLIFF BRUNT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: After the milestone was first wiped out by an offsides review, MacKinnon finally earns 1,000th point
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Nathan MacKinnon appeared to have reached the 1,000-point milestone in the first period of Colorado's game against Chicago. His teammates even streamed onto the ice to celebrate his history-making assist. That turned out to be just a dress rehearsal.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Luka, Lakers frustrated with officiating and themselves in a rough first game without LeBron James
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — LeBron James sat on the end of the Lakers' bench, wearing a red baseball cap while watching his team in the first game since his groin injury. The red on the court Monday night was in Luka Doncic's face, frustrated by his rocky play and angry with the officiating.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Paige Bueckers and UConn are healthy and rolling as her final March Madness approaches
item.description: UNCASVILLE, Conn. (AP) — Paige Bueckers is in a class by herself in the Big East, becoming the first player to win the most outstanding player of the conference tournament three times.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jets and QB Justin Fields agree to a 2-year, $40 million deal, AP source says
item.description: Justin Fields was passed over by the New York Jets four years ago in the NFL draft. Now he's expected to be their No. 1 quarterback. Fields and the Jets agreed Monday to a two-year contract worth $40 million, a person with knowledge of the deal told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK Jr., Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Eagles 'enthusiastically accepted' President Trump's White House invite after Super Bowl win, official says
item.description: The Philadelphia Eagles have "enthusiastically accepted" President Trump's White House invite to celebrate the team's Super Bowl LIX victory.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gerrit Cole will miss 2025 MLB season as Yankees announce ace will undergo Tommy John surgery
item.description: The New York Yankees' worst fears came true, as they announced Gerrit Cole will undergo Tommy John surgery on Tuesday to repair a torn UCL, rendering him out for the entire 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cavaliers rely on scoring balance to beat Bucks 112-100 for 14th consecutive victory
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Max Strus had 17 points to lead a balanced scoring attack as the NBA-leading Cleveland Cavaliers defeated the Milwaukee Bucks 112-100 on Sunday night for their 14th consecutive victory.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: George, Filipowski score 25 in Jazz loss to Sixers
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (ABC4 Sports) - Keyonte George and Kyle Filipowski each scored 25 points, but the Utah Jazz dropped its fifth straight game, losing at Philadelphia Sunday night, 126-122. Lonnie Walker IV and Quentin Grimes scored 25 points apiece for the victories 76ers.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: DK Metcalf is leaving Seattle for Pittsburgh with a new deal in tow, AP sources say
item.description: The Seattle Seahawks are sending two-time Pro Bowler D.K. Metcalf to Pittsburgh for at least a second-round pick, sources with knowledge of the deal told The Associated Press on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Myles Garrett becomes highest-paid non-quarterback in NFL history
item.description: Garrett, the 2023 AP Defensive Player of the Year, had asked for a trade last month but the Browns were adamant about keeping the 29-year-old star in Cleveland.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bowling Green hires former NFL star running back Eddie George as coach
item.description: Bowling Green hired former NFL running back and Tennessee State coach Eddie George on Sunday as the Falcons' head coach.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Soldier Field to host Premier League doubleheader this summer
item.description: The Premier League Summer Series is coming to Chicago this summer, featuring four Premier League clubs - AFC Bournemouth, Everton, Manchester United, and West Ham United - in a doubleheader at Soldier Field on Wednesday, July 30.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Myles Garrett becomes the highest-paid non-quarterback in NFL history at $40 million per year
item.description: Myles Garrett is staying in Cleveland after the Browns gave him a record four-year contract extension that makes the four-time All-Pro edge rusher the highest-paid non-quarterback in NFL history.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Texas district attorney declines to pursue charges against Kansas City Chiefs wide receiver Xavier Worthy
item.description: Xavier Worthy was arrested Friday and has since been released after the county's district attorney said he would not accept the case at this time.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: BYU pulls away from Utah for 8th straight win, 85-74
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - The BYU basketball team is rolling into the postseason. Dawson Baker scored 15 points and Richie Saunders added 14 to lead No. 23 BYU to an 85-74 victory over rival Utah on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
